name: Benne (Sesame Seed) Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Known as sesame seeds in most of the country, "benne" is the name southerners learned to call these seeds, which were brought from Africa by slaves. Toasting benne seeds develops their flavor and also gives these cookies a slightly crunchy texture.
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Toast benne seeds on an ungreased baking sheet until light brown, about 10 to 12 minutes. Watch closely so they don't burn.
3. In a large bowl, mix all ingredients together.
4. Drop dough by half teaspoonfuls, about 1 1/2 inches apart, onto a greased baking sheet.
5. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for light brown cookies, about 4 to 6 minutes.
6. Let cool for about 2 minutes before removing from the baking sheets to a wire rack. Allow to cool completely.
7. Store in a tightly covered cookie tin when thoroughly cooled. Yield: about 6 dozen.
